name: ARTS at Marks Garage
tagline: Honolulu's community arts hub — rotating exhibitions, makers' markets, and the heartbeat of Chinatown's First Friday scene.
bestTime: First Friday of every month for the art walk; regular gallery hours during the week
talkStory: Founded in 2001 in a converted auto repair shop on Hotel Street, ARTS at Marks Garage was a deliberate effort to revitalize Chinatown through the arts and has become Honolulu's most important grassroots creative space. The nonprofit survives on community donations and volunteer energy — every dollar you spend here supports local artists directly. First Friday nights transform the block into a street festival with live music, open studios, and food vendors spilling onto Bethel Street.
